case_title: MARCO GARCIA VS. FEKERTE HYESUS, AN INDIVIDUAL DBA MELANTHA OFFICE

Notice Of Motion And Motion Objecting To Amount Of Deposit In Lieu Of An Undertaking
Off Calendar Feb-26-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: THIRD PARTY CLAIMANT HANNA TESFAYE's Motion Objecting To Amount Of Deposit In Lieu Of An Undertaking.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Third Party Claimant Hanna Tesfaye's "Motion Objecting to Amount of Deposit in Lieu of an Undertaking" is granted in part. Creditor is ordered to increase the bond/deposit to a total of $40,000. (See C.C.P. 720.160(c)(2); 720.710; 995.920(b); CCP 995.960.) On the one hand, the bond should be increased to protect the Third Party Claimant given her present residence and the difficulty she would encounter in recovering the funds if her third party claim is granted. On the other hand, Third Party's evidentiary showing (without bank records) is not entirely compelling. The Court therefore deems the $40,000 bond fair and equitable given the circumstances. Creditor Schulman shall provide the bond by March 12, 2021.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption
Off Calendar Jan-14-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via ZOOM Video-conferencing:

Attorney, John F. Bradley (Email: john@jfbatlaw.Com) of the law offices of John F. Bradley (Tel: 408-998-2000) appears as counsel for judgment creditor assignee/objecting party, Mark Schulman, as trustee of the Mark Schulman 401 (k) Profit Sharing Pian

Attorney, J. Scott Isherwood (Email: scott.isherwood@sbcglobal.net), of the Law Offices of J. Scott Isherwood (Tel: 925.956.1408) appears as counsel for judgment debtor/exemption claimant, Fekerte Hyesus, an individual dba Meiantha Office Cleaning

On calendar:
ASSIGNEE MARK SCHULMAN's Notice Of Hearing Re Judgment Creditor's Claim Of Exemption

The merits of the claim of exemption are not argued but rather an issue raised by exemption claimant counsel is discussed. A third party, Fekerte Hyesus' sister, living in Addis Ababa, capital city of Ethiopia, is purportedly co-owner of the account levied. Assignee and exemption claimant are in discussions regarding this claim. Third party claims are not currently at issue before the Court in this matter.

The claim of exemption is DENIED. Written order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
